# QA Prompt Examples Guide

## Overview

- __Clean and Simple Formatting__

### Purpose

- Provide structured examples of prompts across common tools and workflows
- Ensure prompts follow QA formatting and writing rules
- Improve clarity, consistency, and usability

### Core Rules

__Clean and Simple Formatting__

- Ensure all content is clear and readable
- Avoid formatting elements that do not transfer well to tools such as ADO
- Maintain consistent structure

__Plain Professional Wording__

- Do not use emojis or emoticons
- Maintain a professional tone
- Avoid informal or casual language

__No Summaries__

- Do not include recap sections
- Provide complete content directly

__No Arrows__

- Do not use arrow symbols or characters

__No Decorative Dividers__

- Do not use repeated symbols for separation

__No Stylized Headers__

- Avoid decorative or non-standard header formats

__No Semicolons__

- Use short, clear sentences
- Do not combine statements

__Avoid Ambiguous Wording__

- Avoid the word "like"
- Avoid phrases such as "is successful"
- Use precise, observable descriptions

__Tester-Friendly Language__

- Use clear, actionable wording
- Write from the tester perspective when applicable

__ADO Compatibility__

- Ensure formatting pastes cleanly into ADO
- Keep text structured and predictable

__Simple Steps and Expected Results__

- Use direct and unambiguous instructions
- Clearly describe observable outcomes

__Default Rule Enforcement__

- Apply all rules unless explicitly overridden

### Excel Formatting Guidance

- Use bold header row with gray background
- Apply auto-fit to columns
- Enable word wrap
- Use top-aligned text
- Apply thin borders
- Freeze top row
- Use Calibri size 11

### Prompt Examples by Tool

__Word__

__Content Creation__

- Draft a report using formal language
- Rewrite text to improve clarity and engagement
- Generate an executive summary

__Formatting__

- Apply a professional report format
- Add a table of contents based on headings
- Convert lists into tables

__Review__

- Check grammar and readability
- Extract key action items
- Convert bullet points into full sentences

__Excel__

__Data Analysis__

- Highlight trends in a dataset
- Create a pivot table summary
- Identify outliers

__Formulas and Automation__

- Generate a formula for growth rate
- Calculate totals by category
- Apply conditional formatting rules

__Charts and Reporting__

- Create a bar chart for comparison
- Generate a summary report
- Extract insights from data

__Outlook__

__Email Drafting__

- Write a follow-up email
- Create an out-of-office reply
- Summarize an email thread

__Scheduling and Tasks__

- Draft a meeting agenda
- Suggest a response to a query
- Extract action items from emails

__Teams__

__Meetings__

- Summarize key decisions
- Generate action items
- Draft follow-up communication

__Chat and Collaboration__

- Summarize chat discussions
- Generate a reply
- Create a team poll

__Planning__

- Create a project plan
- Identify discussion points
- Schedule meetings

__General Prompts__

- Identify stakeholders for a project
- Convert a document into a presentation
- Review text for inconsistencies
- Create a value proposition
- Summarize a presentation
- Provide project updates and action items

### Formatting Requirements

- Use bold and underlined section titles only
- Keep all sections the same size
- Use bullet points for all content
- Avoid long paragraphs

## Goal

- Define the primary objective clearly.

## Input

- [SOURCE_TEXT]
- [TARGET_FILE]
- [CONTEXT]

## Constraints

- Be explicit and deterministic.
- Do not assume missing facts.

## Expected Output

- Format: bullet list
- Include actionable results
